# Personal Site Redesign Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Build a retro-modern personal website and blog for Chuck McAndrew using Astro + Tailwind v4 + DaisyUI v5, deployed to GitHub Pages.

**Architecture:** Static site generated by Astro with content collections for blog posts and experience entries. Tailwind v4 with a custom DaisyUI theme provides the retro-terminal dark aesthetic. All pages are pre-rendered HTML with zero client-side JS except a mobile nav toggle.

**Tech Stack:** Astro 5, Tailwind CSS v4, DaisyUI v5, Shiki (syntax highlighting), GitHub Actions (deployment)

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-24-personal-site-redesign-design.md`

---

## File Map

```
src/
  content.config.ts              # Astro content collection schemas (blog, experience)
  content/
    blog/
      hello-world.md             # Placeholder post for development
    experience/
      openteams.md               # Current role
      quansight.md
      adhoc-staff.md
      adhoc-senior.md
      lebanon-libraries.md
      uw-it-specialist.md
      uw-data-intern.md
  pages/
    index.astro                  # Homepage
    about.astro                  # About page
    blog/
      index.astro                # Blog list with tag filtering
      [slug].astro            # Blog post template
    experience.astro             # Full experience timeline
    contact.astro                # Contact links
    rss.xml.ts                   # RSS feed endpoint
  components/
    Nav.astro                    # Navigation bar with logo + links + mobile menu
    Footer.astro                 # Footer with links
    BlogCard.astro               # Blog post card for list/homepage
    ExperienceEntry.astro        # Single experience timeline entry
    Logo.astro                   # SVG logo component (nav, favicon, full sizes)
    SectionDivider.astro         # Box-drawing character divider
    TagList.astro                # Tag display for blog posts
  layouts/
    Base.astro                   # HTML shell, meta tags, fonts, skip link
    Post.astro                   # Blog post layout (extends Base)
  styles/
    global.css                   # Tailwind v4 imports, DaisyUI theme, custom utilities
public/
  favicon.svg                    # SVG favicon
  CNAME                          # GitHub Pages custom domain
astro.config.mjs                 # Astro configuration
tailwind.config.ts               # NOT USED (Tailwind v4 uses CSS config)
package.json
tsconfig.json
.github/
  workflows/
    deploy.yml                   # GitHub Actions deploy to Pages
```

---

## Task 1: Scaffold Astro project and clean up Hugo files

**Files:**
- Remove: `hugo.toml`, `go.mod`, `go.sum`, `hugo_stats.json`, `hugo.disablemenu.toml`, `package.hugo.json`, `.hugo_build.lock`, `.prettierrc`
- Remove directories: `archetypes/`, `assets/`, `content/`, `data/`, `i18n/`, `layouts/`, `themes/`, `public/`, `resources/`, `static/`, `node_modules/`
- Create: `astro.config.mjs`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `src/pages/index.astro`
- Modify: `.gitignore`

- [ ] **Step 1: Remove all Hugo files and directories**

```bash
cd /home/chuck/devel/chuckmcandrew.com
rm -f hugo.toml go.mod go.sum hugo_stats.json hugo.disablemenu.toml package.hugo.json .hugo_build.lock .prettierrc package.json package-lock.json
rm -rf archetypes assets content data i18n layouts themes public resources static node_modules
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create new Astro project in-place**

```bash
cd /home/chuck/devel/chuckmcandrew.com
npm create astro@latest . -- --template minimal --install --no-git --typescript strict
```

The `--no-git` flag preserves existing git repo. The `--typescript strict` flag enables TypeScript with strict mode.

- [ ] **Step 3: Update .gitignore for Astro**

Replace contents of `.gitignore` with:

```
# Astro
dist/
.astro/

# Node
node_modules/

# Environment
.env
.env.*

# Editor
.vscode/
.idea/

# OS
.DS_Store
Thumbs.db

# Superpowers brainstorm files
.superpowers/

# Claude Code guidance
CLAUDE.md
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify Astro runs**

```bash
npm run dev
```

Expected: Dev server starts, shows blank minimal page at http://localhost:4321

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "Replace Hugo with Astro minimal scaffold"
```

---

## Task 2: Install Tailwind v4 + DaisyUI v5 and define custom theme

**Files:**
- Modify: `astro.config.mjs`
- Create: `src/styles/global.css`
- Modify: `src/pages/index.astro` (import global CSS)

- [ ] **Step 1: Install Tailwind CSS v4 for Astro**

```bash
npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Install DaisyUI v5**

```bash
npm install daisyui
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Configure Astro to use Tailwind vite plugin**

Update `astro.config.mjs`:

```js
import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config';
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite';

export default defineConfig({
  site: 'https://chuckmcandrew.com',
  output: 'static',
  vite: {
    plugins: [tailwindcss()],
  },
});
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Create global.css with DaisyUI theme**

Create `src/styles/global.css`:

```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@plugin "daisyui";

@plugin "daisyui/theme" {
  name: "retro-terminal";
  default: true;
  --color-base-100: oklch(from #0f0f1a l c h);
  --color-base-200: oklch(from #1c1c30 l c h);
  --color-base-300: oklch(from #252542 l c h);
  --color-base-content: oklch(from #f0f0f5 l c h);
  --color-primary: oklch(from #fbbf24 l c h);
  --color-primary-content: oklch(from #0f0f1a l c h);
  --color-secondary: oklch(from #b8b8cc l c h);
  --color-secondary-content: oklch(from #0f0f1a l c h);
  --color-accent: oklch(from #fbbf24 l c h);
  --color-accent-content: oklch(from #0f0f1a l c h);
  --color-neutral: oklch(from #1c1c30 l c h);
  --color-neutral-content: oklch(from #f0f0f5 l c h);
  --border-radius-box: 0.5rem;
  --border-radius-btn: 0.25rem;
  --border-radius-badge: 0.25rem;
}

/* Custom CSS variables for values not covered by DaisyUI */
@theme {
  --color-surface-border: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06);
  --color-accent-dim: rgba(251, 191, 36, 0.12);
  --color-text-secondary: #b8b8cc;
  --color-divider: rgba(251, 191, 36, 0.25);

  --font-mono: 'Fira Code', monospace;
  --font-sans: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
}

/* Base styles */
body {
  font-family: var(--font-sans);
}

/* Reduced motion */
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  *, *::before, *::after {
    animation-duration: 0.01ms !important;
    animation-iteration-count: 1 !important;
    transition-duration: 0.01ms !important;
  }
}

/* Focus visible styles */
:focus-visible {
  outline: 2px solid #fbbf24;
  outline-offset: 2px;
}
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Update index.astro to import the CSS**

```astro
---
import '../styles/global.css';
---

<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width" />
    <title>Chuck McAndrew</title>
  </head>
  <body class="bg-base-100 text-base-content">
    <h1 class="font-mono text-primary text-4xl p-8">chuckMcAndrew</h1>
    <p class="text-text-secondary px-8">Theme is working if this text is amber and the background is dark navy.</p>
    <button class="btn btn-primary mx-8 mt-4">DaisyUI Button</button>
  </body>
</html>
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Verify theme renders correctly**

```bash
npm run dev
```

Expected: Dark navy background, amber heading in monospace, DaisyUI button in amber with dark text. Check http://localhost:4321

- [ ] **Step 7: Commit**

```bash
git add src/styles/global.css astro.config.mjs src/pages/index.astro package.json package-lock.json
git commit -m "Add Tailwind v4, DaisyUI v5, and retro-terminal theme"
```

## Task 3: Create Base layout, Logo component, and Nav

**Files:**
- Create: `src/layouts/Base.astro`
- Create: `src/components/Logo.astro`
- Create: `src/components/Nav.astro`
- Create: `public/favicon.svg`
- Modify: `src/pages/index.astro` (use Base layout)

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the SVG favicon**

Create `public/favicon.svg`:

```svg
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 32 32">
  <rect width="32" height="32" rx="4" fill="#0f0f1a"/>
  <rect x="3" y="3" width="26" height="26" rx="3" fill="none" stroke="#fbbf24" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.25"/>
  <text x="16" y="21" text-anchor="middle" font-family="monospace" font-weight="700" font-size="16" fill="#fbbf24">CM</text>
</svg>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create Logo.astro component**

Create `src/components/Logo.astro`:

```astro
---
interface Props {
  size?: 'nav' | 'full' | 'favicon';
  class?: string;
}

const { size = 'nav', class: className } = Astro.props;

const dimensions = {
  nav: { width: 46, height: 28 },
  full: { width: 120, height: 120 },
  favicon: { width: 32, height: 32 },
};

const { width, height } = dimensions[size];
---

{size === 'nav' && (
  <svg
    width={width}
    height={height}
    viewBox="0 0 48 28"
    xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
    class={className}
    aria-hidden="true"
  >
    <rect x="0" y="1" width="46" height="26" rx="4" fill="none" stroke="#fbbf24" stroke-width="1.5" opacity="0.25" />
    <text x="23" y="20" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Fira Code', monospace" font-weight="700" font-size="16" fill="#fbbf24">CM</text>
  </svg>
)}

{size === 'full' && (
  <svg
    width={width}
    height={height}
    viewBox="0 0 120 120"
    xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
    class={className}
    aria-hidden="true"
  >
    <rect width="120" height="120" rx="12" fill="#0f0f1a" />
    <rect x="12" y="12" width="96" height="96" rx="6" fill="none" stroke="#fbbf24" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.2" />
    <text x="22" y="42" font-family="'Fira Code', monospace" font-weight="400" font-size="16" fill="#fbbf24" opacity="0.5">$</text>
    <text x="60" y="72" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Fira Code', monospace" font-weight="700" font-size="52" fill="#fbbf24">CM</text>
    <rect x="85" y="54" width="3" height="24" fill="#fbbf24" opacity="0.7">
      <animate attributeName="opacity" values="0.7;0;0.7" dur="1.2s" repeatCount="indefinite" />
    </rect>
  </svg>
)}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create Nav.astro component**

Create `src/components/Nav.astro`:

```astro
---
import Logo from './Logo.astro';

interface Props {
  currentPath?: string;
}

const { currentPath = '' } = Astro.props;

const links = [
  { href: '/', label: 'home' },
  { href: '/about', label: 'about' },
  { href: '/blog', label: 'blog' },
  { href: '/experience', label: 'experience' },
  { href: '/contact', label: 'contact' },
];

function isActive(href: string, current: string): boolean {
  if (href === '/') return current === '/';
  return current.startsWith(href);
}
---

<nav class="bg-base-200 border-b border-surface-border" aria-label="Main navigation">
  <div class="max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6 py-3 flex justify-between items-center">
    <a href="/" class="flex items-center gap-3 no-underline" aria-label="Home">
      <Logo size="nav" />
      <span class="font-mono font-bold text-primary hidden sm:inline">chuckMcAndrew</span>
    </a>

    <!-- Desktop nav -->
    <ul class="hidden md:flex gap-6 list-none m-0 p-0">
      {links.map(({ href, label }) => (
        <li>
          <a
            href={href}
            class:list={[
              'font-mono text-sm no-underline transition-colors',
              isActive(href, currentPath) ? 'text-primary' : 'text-text-secondary hover:text-primary',
            ]}
            aria-current={isActive(href, currentPath) ? 'page' : undefined}
          >
            {label}
          </a>
        </li>
      ))}
    </ul>

    <!-- Mobile nav toggle -->
    <div class="dropdown dropdown-end md:hidden">
      <label tabindex="0" class="btn btn-ghost text-primary" aria-label="Open menu">
        <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="h-5 w-5" fill="none" viewBox="0 0 24 24" stroke="currentColor">
          <path stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-width="2" d="M4 6h16M4 12h16M4 18h16" />
        </svg>
      </label>
      <ul tabindex="0" class="dropdown-content menu bg-base-200 border border-surface-border rounded-box w-52 p-2 shadow-lg z-50">
        {links.map(({ href, label }) => (
          <li>
            <a
              href={href}
              class:list={[
                'font-mono text-sm',
                isActive(href, currentPath) ? 'text-primary' : 'text-text-secondary',
              ]}
            >
              {label}
            </a>
          </li>
        ))}
      </ul>
    </div>
  </div>
</nav>
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Create Base.astro layout**

Create `src/layouts/Base.astro`:

```astro
---
import '../styles/global.css';
import Nav from '../components/Nav.astro';
import Footer from '../components/Footer.astro';

interface Props {
  title: string;
  description?: string;
}

const { title, description = 'Personal website of Chuck McAndrew, Director of Infrastructure Engineering.' } = Astro.props;
const currentPath = Astro.url.pathname;
---

<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="utf-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
    <meta name="description" content={description} />
    <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg" />
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin />
    <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Fira+Code:wght@400;600;700&family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet" />
    <link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" title="Chuck McAndrew's Blog" href="/rss.xml" />
    <meta property="og:title" content={title} />
    <meta property="og:description" content={description} />
    <meta property="og:type" content="website" />
    <title>{title}</title>
  </head>
  <body class="bg-base-100 text-base-content min-h-screen flex flex-col">
    <a href="#main-content" class="sr-only focus:not-sr-only focus:absolute focus:top-2 focus:left-2 focus:z-50 font-mono text-sm bg-primary text-primary-content px-4 py-2 rounded">
      Skip to content
    </a>
    <Nav currentPath={currentPath} />
    <main id="main-content" class="flex-1">
      <slot />
    </main>
    <Footer />
  </body>
</html>
```

Note: Footer component will be created in the next step. For now, create a placeholder.

## Task 4: Create content collections (blog + experience) with schemas

**Files:**
- Create: `src/content.config.ts`
- Create: `src/content/blog/hello-world.md`
- Create: `src/content/experience/openteams.md`
- Create: `src/content/experience/quansight.md`
- Create: `src/content/experience/adhoc-staff.md`
- Create: `src/content/experience/adhoc-senior.md`
- Create: `src/content/experience/lebanon-libraries.md`
- Create: `src/content/experience/uw-it-specialist.md`
- Create: `src/content/experience/uw-data-intern.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create content collection config**

Create `src/content.config.ts`:

```ts
import { defineCollection, z } from 'astro:content';
import { glob } from 'astro/loaders';

const blog = defineCollection({
  loader: glob({ pattern: '**/*.{md,mdx}', base: './src/content/blog' }),
  schema: z.object({
    title: z.string(),
    date: z.coerce.date(),
    description: z.string(),
    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    draft: z.boolean().default(false),
  }),
});

const experience = defineCollection({
  loader: glob({ pattern: '**/*.md', base: './src/content/experience' }),
  schema: z.object({
    title: z.string(),
    company: z.string(),
    location: z.string(),
    startDate: z.string(),
    endDate: z.string(),
    tech: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    order: z.number(),
  }),
});

export const collections = { blog, experience };
```

## Task 5: Build reusable components (BlogCard, ExperienceEntry, SectionDivider, TagList)

**Files:**
- Create: `src/components/BlogCard.astro`
- Create: `src/components/ExperienceEntry.astro`
- Create: `src/components/SectionDivider.astro`
- Create: `src/components/TagList.astro`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create SectionDivider.astro**

Create `src/components/SectionDivider.astro`:

```astro
<div class="max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6 font-mono text-xs text-divider overflow-hidden whitespace-nowrap" aria-hidden="true">
  ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
</div>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create TagList.astro**

Create `src/components/TagList.astro`:

```astro
---
interface Props {
  tags: string[];
  linkable?: boolean;
}

const { tags, linkable = false } = Astro.props;
---

<div class="flex flex-wrap gap-1.5">
  {tags.map((tag) =>
    linkable ? (
      <a
        href={`/blog?tag=${encodeURIComponent(tag)}`}
        class="font-mono text-xs text-primary bg-accent-dim px-2 py-0.5 rounded no-underline hover:bg-primary hover:text-primary-content transition-colors"
      >
        {tag}
      </a>
    ) : (
      <span class="font-mono text-xs text-primary bg-accent-dim px-2 py-0.5 rounded">
        {tag}
      </span>
    )
  )}
</div>
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create BlogCard.astro**

Create `src/components/BlogCard.astro`:

```astro
---
import TagList from './TagList.astro';

interface Props {
  title: string;
  date: Date;
  description: string;
  tags: string[];
  slug: string;
  readTime?: number;
}

const { title, date, description, tags, slug, readTime } = Astro.props;

const formattedDate = date.toISOString().split('T')[0];
---

<a href={`/blog/${slug}/`} class="block no-underline group">
  <article class="bg-base-200 border border-surface-border rounded-lg p-5 transition-colors group-hover:border-primary/30">
    <div class="font-mono text-xs text-text-secondary mb-1.5">
      {formattedDate}{readTime ? ` - ${readTime} min read` : ''}
    </div>
    <h3 class="font-sans text-base font-semibold text-base-content mb-1.5 group-hover:text-primary transition-colors">
      {title}
    </h3>
    <p class="text-sm text-text-secondary leading-relaxed mb-2.5">
      {description}
    </p>
    <TagList tags={tags} />
  </article>
</a>
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Create ExperienceEntry.astro**

Create `src/components/ExperienceEntry.astro`:

```astro
---
import TagList from './TagList.astro';

interface Props {
  title: string;
  company: string;
  startDate: string;
  endDate: string;
  tech: string[];
  description?: string;
  compact?: boolean;
}

const { title, company, startDate, endDate, tech, description, compact = false } = Astro.props;
---

<div class="relative border-l-2 border-primary/20 pl-5 pb-6 last:pb-0">
  <div class="absolute -left-[5px] top-1.5 w-2 h-2 bg-primary rounded-full" aria-hidden="true"></div>
  <div class="font-mono text-xs text-text-secondary">
    {startDate} - {endDate}
  </div>
  <h3 class="font-sans text-base font-semibold text-base-content mt-1 mb-0.5">
    {title}
  </h3>
  <div class="text-sm text-primary">
    {company}
  </div>
  {!compact && description && (
    <p class="text-sm text-text-secondary leading-relaxed mt-2">
      {description}
    </p>
  )}
  {!compact && tech.length > 0 && (
    <div class="mt-2">
      <TagList tags={tech} />
    </div>
  )}
</div>
```

## Task 6: Build the Homepage

**Files:**
- Modify: `src/pages/index.astro`

- [ ] **Step 1: Build the full homepage**

Update `src/pages/index.astro`:

```astro
---
import Base from '../layouts/Base.astro';
import BlogCard from '../components/BlogCard.astro';
import ExperienceEntry from '../components/ExperienceEntry.astro';
import SectionDivider from '../components/SectionDivider.astro';
import { getCollection } from 'astro:content';

const allPosts = await getCollection('blog', ({ data }) => data.draft !== true);
const recentPosts = allPosts
  .sort((a, b) => b.data.date.valueOf() - a.data.date.valueOf())
  .slice(0, 3);

const allExperience = await getCollection('experience');
const recentExperience = allExperience
  .sort((a, b) => a.data.order - b.data.order)
  .slice(0, 3);

function getReadTime(body: string | undefined): number {
  if (!body) return 1;
  const words = body.trim().split(/\s+/).length;
  return Math.max(1, Math.ceil(words / 200));
}
---

<Base title="Chuck McAndrew">
  <!-- Hero -->
  <section class="max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6 pt-16 pb-12">
    <p class="font-mono text-sm text-primary mb-4">$ whoami</p>
    <h1 class="text-4xl font-bold mb-2">Chuck McAndrew</h1>
    <p class="text-lg text-text-secondary mb-6">Director of Infrastructure Engineering</p>
    <p class="text-text-secondary leading-relaxed mb-8 max-w-xl">
      I build resilient cloud-native systems, lead engineering teams, and contribute to open source. Currently at OpenTeams. Based in Granada, Spain.
    </p>
    <div class="flex gap-3">
      <a href="/blog" class="btn btn-primary font-mono text-sm">read the blog</a>
      <a href="/about" class="btn btn-outline btn-primary font-mono text-sm">about me</a>
    </div>
  </section>

  <SectionDivider />

  <!-- Recent Posts -->
  <section class="max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6 py-10" aria-labelledby="recent-posts">
    <h2 id="recent-posts" class="font-mono text-base text-primary mb-6">
      ~/blog <span class="font-normal text-text-secondary">- recent posts</span>
    </h2>
    {recentPosts.length > 0 ? (
      <div class="flex flex-col gap-3">
        {recentPosts.map((post) => (
          <BlogCard
            title={post.data.title}
            date={post.data.date}
            description={post.data.description}
            tags={post.data.tags}
            slug={post.id}
            readTime={getReadTime(post.body)}
          />
        ))}
      </div>
    ) : (
      <p class="text-text-secondary font-mono text-sm">No posts yet. Check back soon.</p>
    )}
  </section>

  <SectionDivider />

  <!-- Experience Preview -->
  <section class="max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6 py-10" aria-labelledby="experience-preview">
    <h2 id="experience-preview" class="font-mono text-base text-primary mb-6">~/experience</h2>
    <div>
      {recentExperience.map((job) => (
        <ExperienceEntry
          title={job.data.title}
          company={job.data.company}
          startDate={job.data.startDate}
          endDate={job.data.endDate}
          tech={job.data.tech}
          compact={true}
        />
      ))}
    </div>
  </section>
</Base>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify homepage renders with real content**

```bash
npm run dev
```

Expected: Homepage with hero, recent posts section (showing hello-world post), experience preview (showing top 3 jobs), dividers, nav, footer.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add src/pages/index.astro
git commit -m "Build homepage with hero, recent posts, and experience preview"
```

## Task 7: Build the Blog list and post pages

**Files:**
- Create: `src/pages/blog/index.astro`
- Create: `src/pages/blog/[slug].astro`
- Create: `src/layouts/Post.astro`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the blog post layout**

Create `src/layouts/Post.astro`:

```astro
---
import Base from './Base.astro';
import TagList from '../components/TagList.astro';

interface Props {
  title: string;
  date: Date;
  description: string;
  tags: string[];
  slug: string;
  prevPost?: { title: string; slug: string } | null;
  nextPost?: { title: string; slug: string } | null;
}

const { title, date, description, tags, slug, prevPost, nextPost } = Astro.props;

const formattedDate = date.toISOString().split('T')[0];
---

<Base title={`${title} - Chuck McAndrew`} description={description}>
  <article class="max-w-[680px] mx-auto px-6 pt-12 pb-16">
    <!-- Breadcrumb -->
    <nav class="font-mono text-xs text-text-secondary mb-5" aria-label="Breadcrumb">
      <a href="/blog" class="text-primary no-underline hover:underline">~/blog</a>
      <span> / </span>
      <span>{slug}</span>
    </nav>

    <!-- Header -->
    <header>
      <h1 class="text-3xl font-bold leading-tight mb-4">{title}</h1>
      <div class="font-mono text-sm text-text-secondary mb-3 flex gap-4">
        <time datetime={date.toISOString()}>{formattedDate}</time>
      </div>
      <div class="mb-8">
        <TagList tags={tags} linkable />
      </div>
    </header>

    <!-- Body -->
    <div class="prose-custom">
      <slot />
    </div>

    <!-- Footer -->
    <div class="mt-12 font-mono text-xs text-divider overflow-hidden whitespace-nowrap" aria-hidden="true">
      ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    </div>

    <!-- Author bio -->
    <div class="mt-8 flex gap-5 items-start bg-base-200 border border-surface-border rounded-lg p-5">
      <div class="w-14 h-14 bg-accent-dim rounded-full flex items-center justify-center flex-shrink-0" aria-hidden="true">
        <span class="font-mono font-bold text-primary">CM</span>
      </div>
      <div>
        <div class="font-sans font-semibold text-base-content mb-1">Chuck McAndrew</div>
        <p class="text-sm text-text-secondary leading-relaxed">
          Director of Infrastructure Engineering at OpenTeams. I write about infrastructure, open source, and the occasional career reflection. Based in Granada, Spain.
        </p>
      </div>
    </div>

    <!-- Prev/Next -->
    {(prevPost || nextPost) && (
      <div class="mt-8 flex gap-4">
        {prevPost ? (
          <a href={`/blog/${prevPost.slug}/`} class="flex-1 bg-base-200 border border-surface-border rounded-md p-4 no-underline hover:border-primary/30 transition-colors">
            <div class="font-mono text-xs text-text-secondary mb-1">&larr; previous</div>
            <div class="text-sm font-semibold text-base-content">{prevPost.title}</div>
          </a>
        ) : <div class="flex-1" />}
        {nextPost ? (
          <a href={`/blog/${nextPost.slug}/`} class="flex-1 bg-base-200 border border-surface-border rounded-md p-4 no-underline hover:border-primary/30 transition-colors text-right">
            <div class="font-mono text-xs text-text-secondary mb-1">next &rarr;</div>
            <div class="text-sm font-semibold text-base-content">{nextPost.title}</div>
          </a>
        ) : <div class="flex-1" />}
      </div>
    )}
  </article>
</Base>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add prose styling to global.css**

Append to `src/styles/global.css`:

```css
/* Blog post prose styling */
.prose-custom {
  font-size: 1rem;
  line-height: 1.8;
  max-width: none;
}

.prose-custom p {
  margin-bottom: 1.25rem;
}

.prose-custom h2 {
  font-family: var(--font-mono);
  font-size: 1.25rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: #fbbf24;
  margin-top: 2.5rem;
  margin-bottom: 1rem;
  padding-bottom: 0.5rem;
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(251, 191, 36, 0.15);
}

.prose-custom h3 {
  font-size: 1.0625rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  margin-top: 1.75rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
}

.prose-custom a {
  color: #fbbf24;
  text-decoration: underline;
  text-decoration-color: rgba(251, 191, 36, 0.3);
  text-underline-offset: 2px;
}

.prose-custom a:hover {
  text-decoration-color: #fbbf24;
}

.prose-custom ul, .prose-custom ol {
  margin: 0 0 1.25rem 1.5rem;
}

.prose-custom li {
  margin-bottom: 0.375rem;
}

.prose-custom blockquote {
  border-left: 3px solid #fbbf24;
  margin: 1.5rem 0;
  padding: 0.75rem 1.25rem;
  background: #1c1c30;
  border-radius: 0 0.375rem 0.375rem 0;
}

.prose-custom blockquote p {
  color: #b8b8cc;
  font-style: italic;
  margin-bottom: 0;
}

.prose-custom code:not(pre code) {
  font-family: var(--font-mono);
  font-size: 0.875rem;
  background: #1c1c30;
  padding: 0.125rem 0.375rem;
  border-radius: 0.1875rem;
  color: #fbbf24;
}

.prose-custom pre {
  background: #1c1c30;
  border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06);
  border-radius: 0.375rem;
  padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem;
  margin: 1.5rem 0;
  overflow-x: auto;
}

.prose-custom pre code {
  background: none;
  padding: 0;
  font-size: 0.8125rem;
  line-height: 1.7;
  color: #f0f0f5;
}

.prose-custom strong {
  font-weight: 600;
}

.prose-custom img {
  border-radius: 0.375rem;
  margin: 1.5rem 0;
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create blog list page**

Create `src/pages/blog/index.astro`:

```astro
---
import Base from '../../layouts/Base.astro';
import BlogCard from '../../components/BlogCard.astro';
import { getCollection } from 'astro:content';

const allPosts = await getCollection('blog', ({ data }) => data.draft !== true);
const sortedPosts = allPosts.sort((a, b) => b.data.date.valueOf() - a.data.date.valueOf());

const allTags = [...new Set(sortedPosts.flatMap((post) => post.data.tags))].sort();

function getReadTime(body: string | undefined): number {
  if (!body) return 1;
  const words = body.trim().split(/\s+/).length;
  return Math.max(1, Math.ceil(words / 200));
}
---

<Base title="Blog - Chuck McAndrew" description="Blog posts about infrastructure, open source, and more.">
  <div class="max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6 py-12">
    <h1 class="font-mono text-base text-primary mb-2">~/blog</h1>
    <p class="text-text-secondary text-sm mb-8">Writing about infrastructure, open source, and the occasional career reflection.</p>

    <!-- Tags (display only, no client-side filtering in v1) -->
    {allTags.length > 0 && (
      <div class="mb-8 flex flex-wrap gap-2" aria-label="Post tags">
        {allTags.map((tag) => (
          <span class="font-mono text-xs px-2 py-1 rounded text-primary bg-accent-dim">
            {tag}
          </span>
        ))}
      </div>
    )}

    <!-- Posts -->
    <div class="flex flex-col gap-3">
      {sortedPosts.map((post) => (
        <BlogCard
          title={post.data.title}
          date={post.data.date}
          description={post.data.description}
          tags={post.data.tags}
          slug={post.id}
          readTime={getReadTime(post.body)}
        />
      ))}
    </div>

    {sortedPosts.length === 0 && (
      <p class="text-text-secondary font-mono text-sm">No posts yet. Check back soon.</p>
    )}
  </div>
</Base>
```

Note: Tag filtering on a static site will need client-side JS or be implemented as a URL param that pages check. For v1, the tag links serve as visual grouping. Full client-side filtering can be added as an enhancement.

- [ ] **Step 4: Create blog post template page**

Create `src/pages/blog/[slug].astro`:

```astro
---
import { getCollection, render } from 'astro:content';
import Post from '../../layouts/Post.astro';

export async function getStaticPaths() {
  const posts = await getCollection('blog', ({ data }) => data.draft !== true);
  const sortedPosts = posts.sort((a, b) => a.data.date.valueOf() - b.data.date.valueOf());

  return sortedPosts.map((post, index) => ({
    params: { slug: post.id },
    props: {
      post,
      prevPost: index > 0 ? { title: sortedPosts[index - 1].data.title, slug: sortedPosts[index - 1].id } : null,
      nextPost: index < sortedPosts.length - 1 ? { title: sortedPosts[index + 1].data.title, slug: sortedPosts[index + 1].id } : null,
    },
  }));
}

const { post, prevPost, nextPost } = Astro.props;
const { Content } = await render(post);
---

<Post
  title={post.data.title}
  date={post.data.date}
  description={post.data.description}
  tags={post.data.tags}
  slug={post.id}
  prevPost={prevPost}
  nextPost={nextPost}
>
  <Content />
</Post>
```

```bash
npm run dev
```

Expected: `/blog` shows list with hello-world post card. `/blog/hello-world` shows full post with breadcrumb, tags, styled prose, author bio, no prev/next (only one post).

```bash
git commit -m "Add blog list page, post template, and prose styling"
```

```astro
          <div class="absolute -left-[5px] top-1.5 w-2 h-2 bg-primary rounded-full" aria-hidden="true"></div>
          <div class="font-mono text-xs text-text-secondary">June 2013</div>
          <h3 class="font-sans text-base font-semibold text-base-content mt-1 mb-0.5">Master of Library & Information Science</h3>
          <div class="text-sm text-primary">University of Washington, Information School</div>
        </div>
        <div class="relative border-l-2 border-primary/20 pl-5">
          <div class="absolute -left-[5px] top-1.5 w-2 h-2 bg-primary rounded-full" aria-hidden="true"></div>
          <div class="font-mono text-xs text-text-secondary">June 2011</div>
          <h3 class="font-sans text-base font-semibold text-base-content mt-1 mb-0.5">Bachelor of Arts, Global Studies</h3>
          <div class="text-sm text-primary">University of Washington, Tacoma</div>
        </div>
      </div>
    </section>
  </div>
</Base>
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create about page**

Create `src/pages/about.astro`:

```astro
---
import Base from '../layouts/Base.astro';
import Logo from '../components/Logo.astro';
---

<Base title="About - Chuck McAndrew" description="The story of how an auto mechanic and Marine became a Director of Infrastructure Engineering.">
  <article class="max-w-[680px] mx-auto px-6 py-12">
    <h1 class="font-mono text-base text-primary mb-8">~/about</h1>

    <div class="prose-custom">
      <p>
        I'm Chuck McAndrew, Director of Infrastructure Engineering at OpenTeams. I live in Granada, Spain with my family.
      </p>

      <p>
        My path to engineering was anything but conventional. Before I ever touched a server, I was an auto mechanic, a sword maker, and a United States Marine. I eventually found my way to the University of Washington where I earned degrees in Global Studies and Library & Information Science.
      </p>

      <p>
        That library science background turned out to be surprisingly relevant. I started as an IT specialist at UW, then spent seven years as the infrastructure engineer at Lebanon Public Libraries in New Hampshire. Libraries taught me that technology exists to serve people, not the other way around. That principle still drives how I build systems.
      </p>

      <p>
        From there, I moved into the private sector. At Ad Hoc, I worked on mission-critical systems for the Library of Congress, transforming their deployment practices from quarterly releases to multiple weekly deployments. At Quansight, I led the Nebari open-source data science platform, building infrastructure that ran across AWS, GCP, and Azure.
      </p>

      <p>
        Now at OpenTeams, I lead infrastructure engineering and help set the technical direction for the organization's cloud and MLOps strategy.
      </p>

      <p>
        I write here about infrastructure, open source, and occasionally the winding road that brought me to where I am. If any of that sounds interesting, check out <a href="/blog">the blog</a>.
      </p>

      <h2>Get in touch</h2>

      <p>
        You can find me on <a href="https://github.com/dcmcand">GitHub</a> and <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/chuck-mcandrew-93194674/">LinkedIn</a>, or email me at <a href="mailto:chuck@chuckmcandrew.com">chuck@chuckmcandrew.com</a>.
      </p>
    </div>
  </article>
</Base>
```

```bash
npm run dev
```

Expected: `/experience` shows full timeline with all jobs and education. `/about` shows prose story with links.

```astro
    <h1 class="font-mono text-base text-primary mb-8">~/contact</h1>

    <div class="space-y-4">
      <a href="mailto:chuck@chuckmcandrew.com" class="flex items-center gap-3 bg-base-200 border border-surface-border rounded-lg p-4 no-underline hover:border-primary/30 transition-colors group">
        <span class="font-mono text-primary text-sm">email</span>
        <span class="text-text-secondary text-sm group-hover:text-base-content transition-colors">chuck@chuckmcandrew.com</span>

      </a>
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Create RSS feed endpoint**

Create `src/pages/rss.xml.ts`:

```ts
import rss from '@astrojs/rss';
import { getCollection } from 'astro:content';
import type { APIContext } from 'astro';

export async function GET(context: APIContext) {
  const posts = await getCollection('blog', ({ data }) => data.draft !== true);

  return rss({
    title: "Chuck McAndrew's Blog",
    description: 'Writing about infrastructure, open source, and more.',
    site: context.site!,
    items: posts
      .sort((a, b) => b.data.date.valueOf() - a.data.date.valueOf())
      .map((post) => ({
        title: post.data.title,
        pubDate: post.data.date,
        description: post.data.description,
        link: `/blog/${post.id}/`,
      })),
    customData: '<language>en-us</language>',
  });
}
```

```bash
npm run dev
```

Expected: `/contact` shows three link cards. `/rss.xml` returns valid XML with the hello-world post.


## Task 10: Configure syntax highlighting and add CNAME

**Files:**
- Modify: `astro.config.mjs`
- Create: `public/CNAME`

- [ ] **Step 1: Configure Shiki syntax highlighting**

Update `astro.config.mjs`:

```js
import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config';
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite';

export default defineConfig({
  site: 'https://chuckmcandrew.com',
  output: 'static',
  vite: {
    plugins: [tailwindcss()],
  },
  markdown: {
    shikiConfig: {
      theme: 'github-dark',
      wrap: true,
    },
  },
});
```

Note: `github-dark` is a good base that works with the dark theme. The amber accents come from our prose CSS, not the Shiki theme. The Shiki theme handles the token-level coloring (keywords, strings, etc.) which aligns well with our palette.

- [ ] **Step 2: Create CNAME file**

Create `public/CNAME`:

```
chuckmcandrew.com
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Verify syntax highlighting on blog post**

```bash
npm run dev
```

Navigate to `/blog/hello-world` and verify the code block has syntax highlighting.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add astro.config.mjs public/CNAME
git commit -m "Configure Shiki syntax highlighting and add CNAME"
```

---

## Task 11: Add GitHub Actions deployment workflow

**Files:**
- Create: `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the workflow file**

```bash
mkdir -p .github/workflows
```

Create `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`:

```yaml
name: Deploy to GitHub Pages

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: "pages"
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: npm
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
      - name: Build Astro
        run: npm run build
      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
        with:
          path: ./dist

  deploy:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
    steps:
      - name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
        id: deployment
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify build succeeds locally**

```bash
npm run build
```

Expected: Build completes without errors, `dist/` directory contains all pages as HTML.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add .github/workflows/deploy.yml
git commit -m "Add GitHub Actions deployment workflow"
```

---

## Task 12: Update CLAUDE.md and final verification

**Files:**
- Modify: `CLAUDE.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Update CLAUDE.md for the new Astro project**

Replace `CLAUDE.md` contents with documentation reflecting the new Astro setup: commands, architecture, content authoring instructions.

- [ ] **Step 2: Full build and link check**

```bash
npm run build
```

Manually verify all pages render: `/`, `/about`, `/blog`, `/blog/hello-world`, `/experience`, `/contact`, `/rss.xml`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Accessibility check**

Run the dev server and use browser devtools or `axe` to verify:
- Skip link works (Tab on page load)
- All pages have proper heading hierarchy
- All interactive elements are keyboard accessible
- No contrast issues

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add CLAUDE.md
git commit -m "Update CLAUDE.md for Astro project"
```
